78	/*
79	** fast track for 'gettable': if 't' is a table and 't[k]' is present,
80	** return 1 with 'slot' pointing to 't[k]' (position of final result).
81	** Otherwise, return 0 (meaning it will have to check metamethod)
82	** with 'slot' pointing to an empty 't[k]' (if 't' is a table) or NULL
83	** (otherwise). 'f' is the raw get function to use.
84	*/
85	#define luaV_fastget(L,t,k,slot,f) \
86	  (!ttistable(t)  \
87	   ? (slot = NULL, 0)  /* not a table; 'slot' is NULL and result is 0 */  \
88	   : (slot = f(hvalue(t), k),  /* else, do raw access */  \
89	      !isempty(slot)))  /* result not empty? */
90	
91	
92	/*
93	** Special case of 'luaV_fastget' for integers, inlining the fast case
94	** of 'luaH_getint'.
95	*/
96	#define luaV_fastgeti(L,t,k,slot) \
97	  (!ttistable(t)  \
98	   ? (slot = NULL, 0)  /* not a table; 'slot' is NULL and result is 0 */  \
99	   : (slot = (l_castS2U(k) - 1u < hvalue(t)->alimit) \
100	              ? &hvalue(t)->array[k - 1] : luaH_getint(hvalue(t), k), \
101	      !isempty(slot)))  /* result not empty? */
102	
103	
104	/*
105	** Finish a fast set operation (when fast get succeeds). In that case,
106	** 'slot' points to the place to put the value.
107	*/
108	#define luaV_finishfastset(L,t,slot,v) \
109	    { setobj2t(L, cast(TValue *,slot), v); \
110	      luaC_barrierback(L, gcvalue(t), v); }

file.content:
#define luaV_rawequalobj(t1,t2)		luaV_equalobj(NULL,t1,t2)


/*
** fast track for 'gettable': if 't' is a table and 't[k]' is present,
** return 1 with 'slot' pointing to 't[k]' (position of final result).
** Otherwise, return 0 (meaning it will have to check metamethod)
** with 'slot' pointing to an empty 't[k]' (if 't' is a table) or NULL
** (otherwise). 'f' is the raw get function to use.
*/
#define luaV_fastget(L,t,k,slot,f) \
  (!ttistable(t)  \
   ? (slot = NULL, 0)  /* not a table; 'slot' is NULL and result is 0 */  \
   : (slot = f(hvalue(t), k),  /* else, do raw access */  \
      !isempty(slot)))  /* result not empty? */


/*
** Special case of 'luaV_fastget' for integers, inlining the fast case
** of 'luaH_getint'.
*/
#define luaV_fastgeti(L,t,k,slot) \
  (!ttistable(t)  \
   ? (slot = NULL, 0)  /* not a table; 'slot' is NULL and result is 0 */  \
   : (slot = (l_castS2U(k) - 1u < hvalue(t)->alimit) \
              ? &hvalue(t)->array[k - 1] : luaH_getint(hvalue(t), k), \
      !isempty(slot)))  /* result not empty? */


/*
** Finish a fast set operation (when fast get succeeds). In that case,
** 'slot' points to the place to put the value.
*/
#define luaV_finishfastset(L,t,slot,v) \
    { setobj2t(L, cast(TValue *,slot), v); \
      luaC_barrierback(L, gcvalue(t), v); }


/*
** Shift right is the same as shift left with a negative 'y'

content.content:
190	// ── TableSlotRef ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191	
192	/// Internal slot reference returned by the "get" family of functions.
193	///
194	/// Replaces the C pattern of returning `const TValue *` which may point into
195	/// either the array part, the hash part, or the static `absentkey` sentinel.
196	/// Callers use this to either read the value or to feed into `finish_set`.
197	///
198	/// PORT NOTE: In C, `nodefromval(v)` recovers the `Node *` from a value pointer
199	/// via C struct-layout pointer arithmetic.  In Rust we carry the hash-node index
200	/// explicitly in `Hash(usize)` so traversal code (`findindex`) can compute the
201	/// linear position without pointer arithmetic.
202	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
203	pub enum TableSlotRef {
204	    /// Key lives in the array part at this 0-based index.
205	    Array(usize),
206	    /// Key lives in the hash part at this 0-based node index.
207	    Hash(usize),
208	    /// Key is absent from the table (C: `&absentkey`).
209	    Absent,
210	}
211	
212	impl TableSlotRef {
213	    // C: isabstkey(slot)
214	    fn is_absent(self) -> bool { matches!(self, TableSlotRef::Absent) }
215	}
216	
217	// ── LuaTable ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
218	
219	/// A Lua table: hybrid array + hash map.
220	///
221	/// C: `struct Table` in `lobject.h`.  The full field mapping is in
222	/// `ANALYSES/types.tsv` under `Table → LuaTable`.
223	///
224	/// # Dummy hash part
225	///
226	/// When a table has no hash part, `self.node` is empty and
227	/// `self.lastfree` is `None`.  This replaces the C idiom of storing a
228	/// pointer to a shared static `dummynode_` and checking `lastfree == NULL`.
229	pub struct LuaTable {
230	    /// Bit-packed: lower bits = absent-metamethod flags; bit 7 = BIT_RAS.
231	    /// C: `lu_byte flags`.
232	    pub flags: TableFlags,
233	    /// `log2` of the hash-part node count.  `1u32 << lsizenode` = capacity.
234	    /// C: `lu_byte lsizenode`.
235	    pub lsizenode: u8,
236	    /// Array-part size hint or real size depending on `flags.is_real_asize()`.
237	    /// C: `unsigned int alimit`.
238	    pub alimit: u32,
239	    /// The array part; 0-based in Rust (Lua index `k` → `self.array[k-1]`).
240	    /// C: `TValue *array`.
241	    pub array: Vec<LuaValue>,
242	    /// The hash part; length is `1 << lsizenode` when not dummy, 0 when dummy.
243	    /// C: `Node *node`.
244	    pub node: Vec<TableNode>,
245	    /// Cursor into `self.node` for free-slot scanning (one-past the last tried
246	    /// position).  `None` when using the dummy hash part.
247	    /// C: `Node *lastfree` (NULL = dummy).
248	    pub lastfree: Option<usize>,
249	    /// Optional metatable.
250	    /// C: `struct Table *metatable`.
251	    pub metatable: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
252	}
253	
254	impl LuaTable {
255	    // ── Predicate helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	    /// `isdummy(t)` — true when the table has no allocated hash part.
258	    #[inline]
259	    pub fn is_dummy(&self) -> bool {
260	        self.lastfree.is_none()
261	    }
262	
263	    /// `sizenode(t)` — nominal hash-part capacity (`1 << lsizenode`).
264	    /// This is the modulo used for hash indexing, not the vec length.
265	    #[inline]
266	    fn sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
267	        1u32 << self.lsizenode
268	    }
269

content.content:
1	//! Lua table implementation (array + hash hybrid).
2	//!
3	//! Port of `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/ltable.c` (995 lines, 28 functions).
4	//! The companion header `ltable.h` is merged here per PORTING.md §1.
5	//!
6	//! # Design overview
7	//!
8	//! Tables keep elements in two parts: an **array part** (`Vec<LuaValue>`,
9	//! 1-based indices 1..=alimit) and a **hash part** (`Vec<TableNode>`,
10	//! sized to a power-of-two).  Non-negative integer keys are candidates for
11	//! the array part; all other keys go into the hash part.
12	//!
13	//! The hash part uses Brent's variation of chained scatter tables.  The key
14	//! invariant: if an element is not in its *main position* (the slot its hash
15	//! maps to), then the colliding element *is* in its own main position.
16	//!
17	//! ## C → Rust structural differences
18	//!
19	//! | C pattern | Rust pattern |
20	//! |---|---|
21	//! | `Node *` (pointer into hash array) | `usize` index into `self.node` |
22	//! | `const TValue *` return from get functions | `TableSlotRef` enum |
23	//! | `&absentkey` sentinel | `TableSlotRef::Absent` |
24	//! | `lastfree == NULL` (dummy) | `lastfree: None` |
25	//! | `dummynode` static | empty `node: vec![]` + `lastfree: None` |
26	//! | `nodefromval(v)` pointer subtraction | index stored in `TableSlotRef::Hash(i)` |
27	//!
28	//! ## `TableSlotRef`
29	//!
30	//! C functions like `luaH_getint` return `const TValue *`, which may point to

220	///
221	/// C: `struct Table` in `lobject.h`.  The full field mapping is in
222	/// `ANALYSES/types.tsv` under `Table → LuaTable`.
223	///
224	/// # Dummy hash part
225	///
226	/// When a table has no hash part, `self.node` is empty and
227	/// `self.lastfree` is `None`.  This replaces the C idiom of storing a
228	/// pointer to a shared static `dummynode_` and checking `lastfree == NULL`.
229	pub struct LuaTable {
230	    /// Bit-packed: lower bits = absent-metamethod flags; bit 7 = BIT_RAS.
231	    /// C: `lu_byte flags`.
232	    pub flags: TableFlags,
233	    /// `log2` of the hash-part node count.  `1u32 << lsizenode` = capacity.
234	    /// C: `lu_byte lsizenode`.
235	    pub lsizenode: u8,
236	    /// Array-part size hint or real size depending on `flags.is_real_asize()`.
237	    /// C: `unsigned int alimit`.
238	    pub alimit: u32,
239	    /// The array part; 0-based in Rust (Lua index `k` → `self.array[k-1]`).
240	    /// C: `TValue *array`.
241	    pub array: Vec<LuaValue>,
242	    /// The hash part; length is `1 << lsizenode` when not dummy, 0 when dummy.
243	    /// C: `Node *node`.
244	    pub node: Vec<TableNode>,
245	    /// Cursor into `self.node` for free-slot scanning (one-past the last tried
246	    /// position).  `None` when using the dummy hash part.
247	    /// C: `Node *lastfree` (NULL = dummy).
248	    pub lastfree: Option<usize>,
249	    /// Optional metatable.
250	    /// C: `struct Table *metatable`.
251	    pub metatable: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
252	}
253	
254	impl LuaTable {
255	    // ── Predicate helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	    /// `isdummy(t)` — true when the table has no allocated hash part.
258	    #[inline]
259	    pub fn is_dummy(&self) -> bool {
260	        self.lastfree.is_none()
261	    }
262	
263	    /// `sizenode(t)` — nominal hash-part capacity (`1 << lsizenode`).
264	    /// This is the modulo used for hash indexing, not the vec length.
265	    #[inline]
266	    fn sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
267	        1u32 << self.lsizenode
268	    }
269	
270	    /// `allocsizenode(t)` — 0 when dummy, else `1 << lsizenode`.
271	    #[inline]
272	    pub fn alloc_sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
273	        if self.is_dummy() { 0 } else { self.sizenode() }
274	    }
275	
276	    /// `isrealasize(t)` accessor; delegates to `TableFlags`.
277	    #[inline]
278	    fn is_real_asize(&self) -> bool { self.flags.is_real_asize() }

delta.text: lua-5.4.7/src/lvm.h:85-89`). If `t` is not a table, returns `Ok(None)` (the "not a
table; check metamethods" path). Otherwise performs a raw get on the table via `LuaTableRefExt::get(k)`; retur
ns `Ok(None)` when the slot is empty (C: `isempty := ttisnil`)
and `Ok(Some(v))` when present. Signature was tightened from generic `<T, K>` to `(&LuaValue, &
LuaValue)` since every existing call site passes `&LuaValue` references — no call

ers needed updating.
- **Inner `todo!()` next to surface:** `LuaTableRefExt::get` (state.rs:437) is

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1172`
- **What `fast_get` does:** Mirrors the C macro `luaV_fastget(L, t, k, slot, luaH_get)` (`reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lvm.h:85-89`). If `t` is not a table, returns `Ok(None)` (the "not a table; check metamethods" path). Otherwise performs a raw get on the table via `LuaTableRefExt::get(k)`; returns `Ok(None)` when the slot is empty (C: `isempty := ttisnil`) and `Ok(Some(v))` when present. Signature was tightened from generic `<T, K>` to `(&LuaValue, &LuaValue)` since every existing call site passes `&LuaValue` references — no callers needed updating.
- **Inner `todo!()` next to surface:** `LuaTableRefExt::get` (state.rs:437) is still a `todo!("phase-b: LuaTable::get")`. As soon as the panic loop drives execution through `fast_get` on an actual table, that's the next stub to bite.
- **Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only).
